title: Adam Schefter explains how ‘Hard Knocks’ finally landed the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots will appear on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' in 2027, following the Seattle Seahawks in 2026.
NFL revised eligibility rules, removing playoff exemptions, allowing Patriots' participation under established coach Mike Vrabel by 2027.
NFL enforced the Patriots' commitment after they postponed involvement, leading to a rare double announcement with Seahawks.

title: Mass. high school pitcher Brody Bumila’s fastball tops 100 mph, draws crowd of MLB scouts
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Bishop Feehan's Brody Bumila hits 100 mph on the radar gun in a game against Hopkinton.
Bumila struck out nine batters in three innings, allowing no hits but hitting three batters.
Brody Bumila, ranked 69th by Baseball America, is committed to the University of Texas for baseball.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (April 3, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Hawksmoor, a British steakhouse, will open its third U.S. location in Boston's Seaport in Fall 2026.
Jazz Urbane Cafe, offering Southern and global cuisine, is set to open in Nubian Square, Roxbury, in May 2026.
NAYA, a fast-casual Lebanese chain, plans to open a location in Framingham by late summer 2026.

title: Daughter runs Boston Marathon to honor dad with rare vision disorder
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: London Tuma is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Team Eye and Ear, inspired by her father's Usher syndrome diagnosis.
Her father, Jon Tuma, was diagnosed with Usher syndrome type 2C, affecting his hearing and vision, complicating daily tasks.
The race supports Mass Eye and Ear's research on Usher syndrome, a condition impacting both hearing and vision in patients.

title: BU rally pushes back on Pride flag removals, calls for policy change
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University students and faculty protested the removal of Pride flags at the Silber Administrative Center.
Organizers delivered a petition with over 2,000 signatures to university leadership, calling for signage policy revisions.
The rally was driven by concerns over LGBTQ community representation and university policies limiting free speech.

title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston-based designer Megan Lam created an MBTA-themed sweater featuring trains from the commuter rail and Orange, Green, and Red lines.
Inspired by local transit, Lam showcased the sweater, which she started knitting in November, on Instagram to positive acclaim.
Lam plans more Boston-themed projects, preferring unique, one-of-a-kind pieces over sharing patterns within the knitting community.

title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emmie Laudano, a Wellesley high school senior, will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
Laudano trained for the marathon as part of her school’s Dreamfar program, regularly running up Heartbreak Hill.
She aims to raise funds for the Tierney Learning Center, supporting educational resources for Boston's youth.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com features a news quiz to test knowledge of Greater Boston's local stories.
The quiz aims to keep participants informed about trending news in the Greater Boston area.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019, oversees the news quiz initiative.

title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins coach Marco Sturm criticized his team for not respecting the shorthanded Florida Panthers in a 2-1 loss.
The Bruins have a tendency to underperform against weaker teams, despite a strong record against top NHL teams.
Boston's playoff odds remain high at 95% despite the loss, with the playoffs beginning in a few weeks.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied of Reading sentenced to eight months for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied imported animal parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, violating the Lacey Act on wildlife trafficking.
He must pay a $75,000 fine and will serve two years of supervised release post-prison.
